title: Add reflection reader

tasks.title: Add bounded reflection list reader
tasks.verification: RED: bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/reflection.test.ts -t "lists bounded reflection summaries" failed because adv_reflection_list did not exist. GREEN: same test passed after implementing bounded reader and summaries.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added read-only `adv_reflection_list` with bounded source-backed summaries, `count`/`total`/`omitted`, `byFrictionCategory`, exact `bySuggestion`, missing-file empty state, category/change/scope/max filters, and compact per-entry plane/friction/highlight/suggestion summaries. Tests cover bounded summaries and empty state.

tasks.title: Add target_path routing for reflection list

tasks.implementation_summary: Added target-path test for `adv_reflection_list` verifying it routes through `withOptionalTargetPathStore`, reads target store reflection paths, returns target entries, and includes untrusted read-only `_projectContext`. The reader implementation already uses target snapshot routing from the bounded reader task.
tasks.notes: Reflection target_path routing coverage checkpointed at 2cafecca8802d75615cd8f7a288ac1f3493568fb.

tasks.title: Extend wisdom readers with target_path
tasks.verification: RED: bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/wisdom-target.test.ts failed because target-path test file did not exist. GREEN: same command passed after adding target_path support and tests for wisdom readers.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added optional `target_path` to `adv_wisdom_list` and `adv_project_wisdom_list`. Both readers now wrap read execution in `withOptionalTargetPathStore`, use `activeStore` for wisdom reads, project wisdom paths, product filtering, and append `_projectContext` for target reads while preserving existing output shapes. Added tests for target project wisdom and aggregate wisdom reads.
tasks.notes: Wisdom target_path reader support checkpointed at 5af449a7413a38c78d58c12350dab7952745bc63.

tasks.title: Register reflection reader public tool surfaces
tasks.verification: RED: bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tool-registry.test.ts -t "registers adv_reflection_list" failed because createToolMap lacked adv_reflection_list. GREEN: registry/frozen-list/CLI-surface targeted tests passed after public registration.
tasks.implementation_summary: Registered `adv_reflection_list` in `createToolMap`, added it to `ADV_TOOL_NAMES`, frozen CLI bridge list, CLI surface matrix, and tool title mapping. Added registry regression proving the tool is present in `createToolMap` and `ADV_TOOL_NAMES`. Targeted registry/CLI contract tests pass.

tasks.title: Verify reflection and wisdom reader compatibility

tasks.implementation_summary: Verified reflection and wisdom reader compatibility. Targeted reflection/wisdom/storage/target-project/registry/CLI/title tests passed (97 tests across 10 files). `pnpm run check` passed after formatting. Acceptance reviewer then fixed one issue: `adv_wisdom_list` with `target_path + changeId` now stays on disk-snapshot store without live Temporal lookup; regression added. Reviewer verification passed wisdom-target tests, scoped reflection/wisdom/registry/CLI tests, and `pnpm run check`.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation: adv_reflection_list + target_path on wisdom readers

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/reflection.ts:307-347
sources.summary: listReflections(projectDir, options) already exists, read-only, returns recency-sorted entries, supports reflectionsPath override + product/repo scope filters, returns [] for missing file, skips malformed/invalid lines via parseReflectionEntries with appendDebugLog degradation. Reader can reuse as-is.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts:371-388
sources.summary: withOptionalTargetPathStore short-circuits to current store when no target_path, else routes through snapshot-ok (createLegacyStore, read-only) and passes formatTargetProjectContext(context) as second arg. Exactly the read-only target routing the design assumes.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1523-1525
sources.summary: Canonical read-tool usage: execute(args, store) wraps body in withOptionalTargetPathStore({store, target_path}, async (activeStore, projectContext) => ...). Reflection reader should mirror this.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/wisdom.ts:226-414
sources.summary: adv_wisdom_list and adv_project_wisdom_list currently take (args, store) with NO target_path arg and NO withOptionalTargetPathStore wrapping. They read store.wisdom.* and listProjectWisdom(store.paths.root,...) plus product filtering via isWisdomVisibleForProductScope / productContextOutput. Adding target_path requires wrapping body and routing all reads through activeStore + emitting projectContext.

sources.label: plugin/src/tool-registry.ts:688,839-911
sources.summary: adv_reflect bound via bindTool(reflectionTools.adv_reflect,...). ADV_TOOL_NAMES is a frozen readonly allowlist; createDegradedToolMap iterates it so a new name is auto-stubbed there.

sources.label: plugin/src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts:122-196
sources.summary: Hard contract: ADV_TOOL_NAMES must .toEqual(frozen) — a literal frozen array. Adding adv_reflection_list to ADV_TOOL_NAMES WITHOUT updating this frozen list fails the test. Both must change together.

sources.label: plugin/src/tool-registry.test.ts:66-103,514-525 + cli-surface-matrix.test.ts:26-28
sources.summary: Drift guards: ADV_TOOL_NAMES must exactly match createToolMap keys; every name needs a non-empty args object; cli-surface-matrix requires a matrix row/disposition for every ADV_TOOL_NAMES entry. New tool must be registered in all three surfaces.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/json.ts:138,173,189
sources.summary: store.paths.reflections is set for both external state (ext/reflections.jsonl) and in-repo (.adv/reflections.jsonl). reflection.ts write tool passes store.paths.external ?? store.paths.root + store.paths.reflections to appendReflection; reader should pass the same projectDir + reflectionsPath override for parity.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is feasible and aligns with existing, proven patterns. listReflections already provides the read-only, bounded-friendly, malformed-tolerant, empty-safe primitive (returns [] for missing file; skips bad lines). withOptionalTargetPathStore is the established read-only snapshot routing used by adv_status/gate/task/change and is exactly fit for a new reflection reader and for retrofitting the two wisdom readers. The aggregate fields (count/total/omitted/byFrictionCategory/bySuggestion) are pure in-tool shaping over ReflectionEntry.plane2 (friction_items[].category and improvement_suggestions) — net-new derived output, no storage change. Biggest non-obvious surface is the tool-registration contract: a frozen ADV_TOOL_NAMES snapshot test plus createToolMap/degraded-map/cli-surface-matrix/args drift guards. Wisdom readers currently take (args, store) with no target_path and read store.* + store.paths.root directly, so retrofitting target_path means wrapping the whole body in withOptionalTargetPathStore and threading activeStore through every read (store.wisdom.search/listAll/list and listProjectWisdom) plus emitting _projectContext — preserving the existing product-scope filtering on the active store.

validation.notes: No architectural conflicts. Design respects read-only/storage-owned-degradation boundaries and reuses canonical target routing. Corrections below are completeness/contract obligations, not design flaws.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to design gate as READY with required corrections folded into the design: (1) Explicitly enumerate ALL tool-registration surfaces the new adv_reflection_list must touch — createToolMap binding (bindTool like adv_reflect), ADV_TOOL_NAMES, the frozen array in cli-bridge-contract.test.ts (.toEqual(frozen)), a cli-surface-matrix row/disposition, a non-empty args object, and a tool-title.ts read(...) entry. Missing any breaks an existing contract/drift test. (2) For the wisdom retrofit, specify that the entire execute body is wrapped in withOptionalTargetPathStore and every read goes through activeStore (store.wisdom.search/listAll/list + listProjectWisdom(activeStore.paths.root, {wisdomPath: activeStore.paths.wisdom})), with _projectContext appended only when target_path is set, and product-scope filtering applied against the active store — guaranteeing output shape is unchanged when target_path is absent. (3) Reader should call listReflections(store.paths.external ?? store.paths.root, {reflectionsPath: store.paths.reflections, ...scope}) to match the write-tool path resolution and resolveReadableReflectionsPath legacy fallback.
follow_ups: Design must define exact bounding semantics for count vs total vs omitted (total = full listReflections length pre-truncation; count = returned slice; omitted = total - count) so the reader is deterministic and testable.

Confirm byFrictionCategory keys are constrained to the FrictionItemSchema enum (tool_gap|workaround|missing_capability|docs_gap|ux_friction|provider_specific) and bySuggestion shape (count of improvement_suggestions vs grouping) in the design to avoid ambiguous test expectations.

When wisdom readers gain target_path, add the targetPathSchema-style arg (target_path optional only; no target_confirmed needed for read-only snapshot) and ensure schemas:generate / schemas:check stays green if these tools have public JSON schemas.

changes_made.summary: Fixed change-specific adv_wisdom_list target_path reads to stay on the read-only disk-snapshot target store instead of attempting a live Temporal workflow query; added regression coverage in plugin/src/tools/wisdom-target.test.ts.
changes_made.verification: bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/wisdom-target.test.ts passed (3 tests); scoped registry/reflection/wisdom/CLI tests passed (54 tests); pnpm run check passed.

wisdom_candidates.content: target_path reader paths that use withOptionalTargetPathStore must avoid helper calls that can re-enter live Temporal workflows; change-specific read branches need an explicit projectContext/snapshot guard when current-project reads normally prefer workflow state.

required_main_agent_actions: Consider promoting the target_path snapshot/Temporal lookup gotcha as project wisdom.
If acceptance requires full-suite green, handle unrelated rq-epicScopeDerivation01/rq-epicMutableScope01 citation invariant failures in their owning scope before release.

wisdom.content: For read-only target_path tools, wrap the reader body in `withOptionalTargetPathStore`, use the active target store for all storage reads and product filtering, and append `_projectContext` only when target context exists. Do not add target confirmation fields for snapshot-only reads.

For target_path snapshot readers with changeId filters, avoid live Temporal workflow lookup against the target project unless the target store is known live/current. Snapshot reads should stay on disk/store fallback so untrusted target reads remain read-only and service-independent.

A new read-only reflection reader (`adv_reflection_list` or equivalent) returns reflection entries sorted newest-first with bounded `entries`, `count`, `byFrictionCategory`, `bySuggestion`, and `omitted` metadata.

The reflection reader supports filters for `changeId`, `scope` (`repo`/`product`), `category`, `maxEntries`, and `target_path` where practical; missing optional filters default to safe bounded current-project reads.

documents.proposal:
# Add cross-project reflection reader

## Why

Cross-project ADV usage reviews need wisdom and reflection data from other projects, but current safe tool surfaces are incomplete. `adv_status` and `adv_change_show target_path` can inspect changes, tasks, and selected wisdom attached to sampled changes, but there is no bounded `target_path`-capable reader for project-level wisdom and archived reflection reports.

## Problem Statement

Agents need a read-only, bounded, target-project-safe way to inspect wisdom/reflection learnings across ADV projects without direct ADV state-file reads or manual sampling of individual changes.

## What Changes

Add a safe reader surface for project wisdom and reflection summaries that supports current project and explicit `target_path` snapshots. The reader should summarize recurring friction, project execution learnings, system friction, and actionable follow-up themes with bounded output and drill-down.

## User Outcomes

- Users can ask "what have we learned from ADV usage in project X?" and get sourced, bounded summaries.
- Agents can mine cross-project friction without violating ADV state access policy.
- Reflections become actionable inputs to roadmap/improvement work rather than write-only logs.
- High-volume projects remain readable through filters, limits, and summary grouping.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Read-only tool/API surface for project-level wisdom and reflection summaries.
- `target_path` support with existing trust/read-only semantics.
- Filters for project/repo scope, change ID, date/recency, type/category, and top-N summaries if practical.
- Bounded response shape with counts, omitted counts, and source references.
- Tests proving no direct state-file reads by agents are needed and untrusted target mutation rules are not invoked for read-only access.
- Documentation/command guidance updates where ADV workflows currently ask agents to inspect wisdom/reflections.

### Out of Scope

- Mutating reflection reports.
- Auto-promoting wisdom to roadmap changes without user/agent decision.
- LLM-only summarization as source of truth; grouping can assist, but returned data must cite structured entries or persisted reports.
- Cross-product global analytics dashboards.

### Must Not

- Must not expose private session/workdir details beyond existing target-path read policy.
- Must not require direct reads of ADV storage paths.
- Must not collapse reflection evidence into unsourced prose.
- Must not mutate target project state during read-only access.

## Error Handling / Fallback

- If target project reflection/wisdom storage is missing or unreadable, return a structured partial-result warning with project context; do not fail the whole request when other sections are readable.
- If `target_path` is untrusted, preserve read-only snapshot behavior and refuse mutation-only fields rather than asking for target confirmation.
- If result volume exceeds budget, return deterministic top-N summaries, omitted counts, and drill-down filters instead of truncating mid-entry.
- If no reflections exist, return an explicit empty state and project wisdom counts, not an error.

## Evidence

Observed during PokeEdge/PokeEdge-Web review: change/task/status data was available through `target_path`, but wisdom/reflection mining required sampling individual changes (`adv_change_show include.subagentReports/wisdom`) and could not read reflection reports as a first-class bounded project view.

Created agenda: `ag-jt_U1E-f Add cross-project reflection reader`.

## Discovery Agenda

- Locate current reflection persistence/read code and project wisdom read paths.
- Determine whether to extend `adv_wisdom_list`, add `target_path` to it, add `adv_reflection_list`, or introduce a combined insights reader.
- Define bounded response schema for reflection plane 1/plane 2 summaries and wisdom categories.
- Validate privacy/trust behavior for `target_path` read snapshots.
- Add tests with multiple archived changes/reflections and project wisdom entries.
documents.problemStatement: ADV reflections and project wisdom are hard to mine across projects because there is no bounded target_path-capable reader. Agents must manually sample changes or risk direct state-file reads, making reflection output less actionable.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — Add cross-project reflection reader

## Discovery Findings

- Reflection storage already has a typed `ReflectionEntry` model and `listReflections(projectDir, options)` read API that returns newest-first entries, supports `changeId`, product/repo filters, malformed-line degradation, and external-state `reflectionsPath` override.
- Current agent-facing reflection tool surface is write-only: `adv_reflect` creates/persists a report for an archived change but there is no `adv_reflection_list` / reader tool.
- Project wisdom has two reader tools: `adv_wisdom_list` and `adv_project_wisdom_list`. They are safe readers but currently have no `target_path` argument, while `adv_status`, `adv_change_show`, gates, and tasks already use `withOptionalTargetPathStore` for read-only target snapshots.
- Target-path read semantics already exist: `withOptionalTargetPathStore(... stateRequirement: "snapshot-ok")` returns `_projectContext` with an untrusted read-only warning and does not require target confirmation.
- Reflection reports are stored outside repo state (`store.paths.reflections`) and must be read through storage/tool APIs, never direct file reads by agents.

## Success Criteria

- Agents can list and summarize reflections for current and target projects without direct ADV state-file reads.
- Agents can list project wisdom from target projects using the same read-only target snapshot semantics as `adv_status`.
- Outputs are bounded, source-backed, and include counts/omitted counts/drill-down enough for cross-project usage reviews.
- Existing `adv_reflect` write behavior and wisdom reader behavior remain backward-compatible.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: A new read-only reflection reader (`adv_reflection_list` or equivalent) returns reflection entries sorted newest-first with bounded `entries`, `count`, `byFrictionCategory`, `bySuggestion`, and `omitted` metadata.
- AC2: The reflection reader supports filters for `changeId`, `scope` (`repo`/`product`), `category`, `maxEntries`, and `target_path` where practical; missing optional filters default to safe bounded current-project reads.
- AC3: When `target_path` is supplied, the reflection reader uses existing target-project snapshot semantics and returns `_projectContext`; untrusted target paths remain read-only and require no mutation confirmation.
- AC4: `adv_project_wisdom_list` supports `target_path` read-only snapshots and returns `_projectContext` for target reads while preserving current output shape (`entries`, `count`, `byType`).
- AC5: `adv_wisdom_list` supports `target_path` for aggregate/query/change-specific reads where current logic allows, using target store reads instead of current store reads.
- AC6: Missing reflection/wisdom files return explicit empty states with counts of 0, not unhandled errors.
- AC7: Malformed reflection lines continue to be skipped/degraded by storage parser; reader returns valid entries and source-backed summaries only.
- AC8: Tests cover current-project and target-path reads for reflection and wisdom surfaces, including untrusted target `_projectContext` behavior without target confirmation.
- AC9: Tests prove existing `adv_reflect`, current-project `adv_project_wisdom_list`, and current-project `adv_wisdom_list` behavior remain backward-compatible.
- AC10: Validation passes with targeted reflection/wisdom/target-project tests and `pnpm run check`.

## Constraints

- No direct ADV state-file reads by agents; implementation may use storage APIs and store paths.
- No mutation during reader calls, including target project reads.
- No LLM-only synthesis as source of truth; summaries must be deterministic counts/excerpts sourced from persisted reflection/wisdom entries.
- Preserve product-linked visibility filtering for wisdom and reflections.
- Keep output bounded with explicit omitted counts rather than truncating mid-entry.

## Out of Scope

- Changing `adv_reflect` report generation semantics.
- Editing or deleting reflection reports.
- Auto-promoting reflections/wisdom into roadmap changes.
- Building a dashboard or cross-product analytics service.
- Rewriting reflection storage format.

## Risks / Design Questions

- Best tool shape: add `adv_reflection_list` and extend wisdom tools, versus a single combined `adv_insights` reader. Discovery favors smaller additive tools first because wisdom readers already exist and reflection storage already exposes `listReflections`.
- `adv_wisdom_list` change-specific target reads may query target workflow handles; design must verify target store handle lookup works under snapshot target stores or choose disk/store fallback safely.
- Category summary should count reflection `plane2.friction_items[].category`; suggestion grouping should remain deterministic and bounded, not semantic clustering.
- CLI/schema/tool-registry exposure must stay aligned if adding a new public tool.
documents.design:
# Design — Add cross-project reflection reader

## Design Summary

Add a small read-only reflection reader tool and extend existing wisdom readers with `target_path` support. Reuse existing storage APIs (`listReflections`, project wisdom readers, `store.wisdom`) and existing target-project snapshot routing (`withOptionalTargetPathStore`) so agents can mine cross-project learnings without direct ADV state-file reads.

Independent validator verdict: **READY**. Required registration/path/wisdom-routing corrections are incorporated below.

## Key Decisions

### D1 — Add `adv_reflection_list` beside `adv_reflect`

Keep `adv_reflect` as the write/generation tool. Add a new read-only tool in `plugin/src/tools/reflection.ts`:

```ts
adv_reflection_list({
  changeId?: string,
  category?: ReflectionFrictionCategory,
  scope?: "repo" | "product",
  maxEntries?: number,
  target_path?: string,
})
```

The tool reads via:

```ts
listReflections(activeStore.paths.external ?? activeStore.paths.root, {
  changeId,
  scope,
  productId,
  originRepoId,
  reflectionsPath: activeStore.paths.reflections,
})
```

This preserves write-path parity and legacy fallback behavior. `target_path` uses `withOptionalTargetPathStore({ store, target_path }, ...)` with `snapshot-ok` semantics. It is read-only and does not require `target_confirmed`.

### D2 — Return bounded source-backed summaries

Return shape:

```ts
{
  entries: ReflectionEntrySummary[],
  count: number,      // returned bounded entries
  total: number,      // filtered entries before truncation
  omitted: number,    // total - count
  byFrictionCategory: Record<ReflectionFrictionCategory, number>,
  bySuggestion: Record<string, number>,
  _projectContext?: TargetProjectOutputContext
}
```

`entries` are bounded by named constants (`DEFAULT_REFLECTION_LIST_LIMIT`, `MAX_REFLECTION_LIST_LIMIT`) and include stable source fields: `id`, `change_id`, `created_at`, optional product/repo origin tags, compact plane1 metrics, bounded `friction_items`, bounded `highlights`, and bounded `improvement_suggestions`.

`byFrictionCategory` counts known `ReflectionEntry` friction category enum values from all filtered entries before entry bounding. `bySuggestion` is exact string counting over `plane2.improvement_suggestions` after trim, sorted by count desc then key asc, and capped with a named top-N constant. No semantic clustering.

### D3 — Extend wisdom readers with target-path routing

Add optional `target_path` to:

- `adv_project_wisdom_list`
- `adv_wisdom_list`

Wrap the whole existing execution body in `withOptionalTargetPathStore`. Thread `activeStore` through every read:

- `activeStore.wisdom.search/listAll/list`
- `listProjectWisdom(activeStore.paths.root, { wisdomPath: activeStore.paths.wisdom })`
- product filtering via `activeStore`, not the caller store

For target reads, return `_projectContext`. Preserve existing output shapes when `target_path` is omitted:

- `adv_project_wisdom_list`: `entries`, `count`, `byType`
- `adv_wisdom_list`: `wisdom`, `count`, `byType`

For `adv_wisdom_list changeId` target reads, prefer the same current logic against the target store. If workflow handle lookup is unavailable under a snapshot target, fall back to `activeStore.wisdom.list(changeId)` as current code already does when no handle exists.

### D4 — Empty/malformed behavior remains storage-owned

Reflection storage already returns `[]` when no file exists and skips malformed lines via `parseReflectionEntries` debug logging. The tool should surface explicit empty counts (`count: 0`, `total: 0`, `omitted: 0`, empty summaries), not convert empty state into errors.

### D5 — Register public tool consistently

Expose `adv_reflection_list` through all public/tool surfaces:

- `reflectionTools` export
- `tool-registry.ts` binding via `createToolMap`
- `ADV_TOOL_NAMES`
- frozen `cli-bridge-contract.test.ts` list
- `cli-surface-matrix` row if applicable
- non-empty args-object tests
- `utils/tool-title.ts` using `read(...)`
- any schema registry/tool asset contract that enumerates public tools

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add reflection list summarization helpers and tests in/near `tools/reflection.ts`.
2. Add `adv_reflection_list` tool with `target_path` support and path parity: `activeStore.paths.external ?? activeStore.paths.root` plus `reflectionsPath` override.
3. Extend `adv_wisdom_list` and `adv_project_wisdom_list` args/execution with `withOptionalTargetPathStore`, using `activeStore` throughout.
4. Register new tool in `tool-registry.ts`, `ADV_TOOL_NAMES`, CLI/title/asset contract surfaces.
5. Add current-project and target-path tests for reflection and wisdom readers.
6. Add regression tests for empty reflection file, malformed reflection degradation, bounded output, and backward compatibility for existing `adv_reflect`/wisdom readers.

## Testing Plan

- RED tests:
  - `adv_reflection_list` returns bounded summaries and `byFrictionCategory` from multiple persisted reflection entries.
  - `adv_reflection_list target_path` returns `_projectContext` and reads target store paths without target confirmation.
  - Missing reflection file returns empty state.
  - Malformed reflection JSONL line is skipped while valid entries remain.
  - `adv_project_wisdom_list target_path` returns target project wisdom and `_projectContext` while current-project shape remains unchanged.
  - `adv_wisdom_list target_path` works for aggregate/query/change-specific reads where possible.
  - Tool registration tests cover `adv_reflection_list` in frozen/allowlist/title surfaces.
- GREEN validation:
  - `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/reflection.test.ts src/storage/reflection.test.ts src/tools/wisdom.test.ts src/tools/target-project.test.ts src/tool-registry.test.ts src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts`
  - `pnpm run check`

## Compatibility Plan

- `adv_reflect` unchanged except shared helpers if any.
- Existing wisdom reader output fields preserved; `target_path` and `_projectContext` are additive.
- Reflection storage format unchanged.
- Target reads use existing read-only snapshot semantics; no target confirmation required.

## Risks

- Tool registration/asset drift: mitigate with explicit registration task and contract tests.
- Product-linked reflection filtering may have edge cases when origin tags are missing. Keep current storage behavior: missing product/repo tags remain visible rather than dropping legacy entries.
- `bySuggestion` can become noisy because suggestions are prose. Keep it exact/source-backed and bounded; do not infer semantic clusters.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Add reflection reader

## Outcome

ADV now has a safe read path for reflection reports and cross-project wisdom mining. Agents can inspect current-project or `target_path` project learnings without direct ADV state-file reads.

## What shipped

- Added read-only `adv_reflection_list` with bounded source-backed summaries.
- Reflection list output includes `entries`, `count`, `total`, `omitted`, `byFrictionCategory`, and exact `bySuggestion` counts.
- Added reflection filters for `changeId`, friction `category`, `scope`, `maxEntries`, and `target_path`.
- Added `target_path` support to `adv_project_wisdom_list` and `adv_wisdom_list` using read-only snapshot routing.
- Registered `adv_reflection_list` across public tool surfaces, frozen tool list, CLI matrix, and tool-title mapping.
- Reviewer remediation: `adv_wisdom_list target_path + changeId` stays on disk snapshot reads instead of live Temporal lookup.

## Verification

- Targeted reflection/wisdom/storage/target-project/registry/CLI/title tests passed: 97 tests across 10 files.
- Reviewer verification passed: wisdom-target regression, scoped reflection/wisdom/registry/CLI tests, and `pnpm run check`.
- `pnpm run check` passed.

## Remaining concerns

- None blocking. `bySuggestion` is exact string counting, intentionally not semantic clustering.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-27T20:45:08.408Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Agents can list and summarize reflections for current and target projects without direct ADV state-file reads. | pass | Implemented read-only `adv_reflection_list` and target_path wisdom readers; agents can list reflection/wisdom learnings through tools rather than direct ADV state-file reads. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Agents can list project wisdom from target projects using the same read-only target snapshot semantics as `adv_status`. | pass | `adv_reflection_list target_path`, `adv_project_wisdom_list target_path`, and `adv_wisdom_list target_path` tests return `_projectContext` and target entries using snapshot routing. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Outputs are bounded, source-backed, and include counts/omitted counts/drill-down enough for cross-project usage reviews. | pass | Reflection list tests assert bounded entries, `total`/`count`/`omitted`, `byFrictionCategory`, exact `bySuggestion`, and source-backed compact entry summaries. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | Existing `adv_reflect` write behavior and wisdom reader behavior remain backward-compatible. | pass | Existing `adv_reflect`, current-project wisdom list behavior, and public registration tests pass; output changes for wisdom are additive only when target_path is supplied. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | A new read-only reflection reader (`adv_reflection_list` or equivalent) returns reflection entries sorted newest-first with bounded `entries`, `count`, `byFrictionCategory`, `bySuggestion`, and `omitted` metadata. | pass | `adv_reflection_list` returns newest-first bounded summaries with `entries`, `count`, `total`, `omitted`, `byFrictionCategory`, and `bySuggestion`; covered by `reflection.test.ts`. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | The reflection reader supports filters for `changeId`, `scope` (`repo`/`product`), `category`, `maxEntries`, and `target_path` where practical; missing optional filters default to safe bounded current-project reads. | pass | Tool args include `changeId`, `category`, `scope`, `maxEntries`, and `target_path` with safe defaults and named max/default bounds. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | When `target_path` is supplied, the reflection reader uses existing target-project snapshot semantics and returns `_projectContext`; untrusted target paths remain read-only and require no mutation confirmation. | pass | `reflection-target.test.ts` proves `adv_reflection_list target_path` uses `withOptionalTargetPathStore`, reads target store paths, and returns untrusted read-only `_projectContext` without confirmation. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_project_wisdom_list` supports `target_path` read-only snapshots and returns `_projectContext` for target reads while preserving current output shape (`entries`, `count`, `byType`). | pass | `wisdom-target.test.ts` proves `adv_project_wisdom_list target_path` reads target project wisdom via activeStore paths and returns `_projectContext` while preserving `entries`, `count`, `byType`. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_wisdom_list` supports `target_path` for aggregate/query/change-specific reads where current logic allows, using target store reads instead of current store reads. | pass | `wisdom-target.test.ts` proves `adv_wisdom_list target_path` aggregate and change-specific paths use target store reads and return `_projectContext`; reviewer fixed changeId path to avoid live Temporal lookup under target snapshots. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Missing reflection/wisdom files return explicit empty states with counts of 0, not unhandled errors. | pass | Reflection list missing-file test returns explicit empty state: `entries: []`, `count: 0`, `total: 0`, `omitted: 0`, empty summaries. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Malformed reflection lines continue to be skipped/degraded by storage parser; reader returns valid entries and source-backed summaries only. | pass | Storage reflection tests still cover malformed-line degradation; reader uses `listReflections`, so malformed entries are skipped by storage and valid entries remain source-backed. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Tests cover current-project and target-path reads for reflection and wisdom surfaces, including untrusted target `_projectContext` behavior without target confirmation. | pass | Targeted tests cover current and target reads for reflection/wisdom surfaces, including untrusted `_projectContext` behavior without target confirmation. |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | Tests prove existing `adv_reflect`, current-project `adv_project_wisdom_list`, and current-project `adv_wisdom_list` behavior remain backward-compatible. | pass | Existing `adv_reflect`, current-project `adv_project_wisdom_list`, and current-project `adv_wisdom_list` tests pass; tool registry/CLI/title contract tests pass. |
| AC10 | acceptance_criterion | Validation passes with targeted reflection/wisdom/target-project tests and `pnpm run check`. | pass | Verification passed: targeted reflection/wisdom/storage/target-project/registry/CLI/title tests; reviewer scoped tests; `pnpm run check`. |
| C1 | constraint | No direct ADV state-file reads by agents; implementation may use storage APIs and store paths. | respected | No agent direct state-file read path added; implementation uses storage APIs (`listReflections`, `listProjectWisdom`, `store.wisdom`). |
| C2 | constraint | No mutation during reader calls, including target project reads. | respected | Reader calls only list/search APIs and target snapshot routing; no write/signal/mutation occurs in `adv_reflection_list` or wisdom list readers. |
| C3 | constraint | No LLM-only synthesis as source of truth; summaries must be deterministic counts/excerpts sourced from persisted reflection/wisdom entries. | respected | Summaries are deterministic counts/excerpts from persisted entries; no LLM synthesis or semantic clustering used. |
| C4 | constraint | Preserve product-linked visibility filtering for wisdom and reflections. | respected | Wisdom product filtering now runs against activeStore; reflection list passes product/repo filters to `listReflections`, preserving legacy visible-missing-tags behavior. |
| C5 | constraint | Keep output bounded with explicit omitted counts rather than truncating mid-entry. | respected | Output uses named bounds and omitted counts for entries/details/suggestions; no mid-entry truncation authority. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Changing `adv_reflect` report generation semantics. | not_applicable | `adv_reflect` generation semantics unchanged except shared imports/helpers; no report-generation redesign. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Editing or deleting reflection reports. | not_applicable | No reflection edit/delete behavior added. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Auto-promoting reflections/wisdom into roadmap changes. | not_applicable | No auto-promotion from reflection/wisdom to roadmap changes implemented. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Building a dashboard or cross-product analytics service. | not_applicable | No dashboard/cross-product analytics service implemented. |
| OOS5 | out_of_scope | Rewriting reflection storage format. | not_applicable | Reflection storage format unchanged; reader reuses existing storage parser/list API. |
